message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #5 done, CI green, need 9/9 consensus">
Task #5 (HTMX inline editing) committed, pushed, CI GREEN (be0e44a, 65 tests).

NOTE: Carson pushed before consensus — this was a process deviation from the updated flow. We'll address this in the final process update to TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md. For now, the code is pushed and CI is green.

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review commit be0e44a and send consent or concerns. This is the last task — we need 9/9 consensus.

Changes:
- New template: todo_item_edit.html (inline edit form fragment)
- Modified: todo_item.html (HTMX attributes on edit link)
- Modified: routes/todos.rs (new get_todo_item handler, HTMX fragment responses for edit)
- Modified: startup.rs (new GET /todos/{id} route)
- Modified: todo-focus.js (focus management for edit mode)
- 4 new integration tests, 65 total
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review this commit thoroughly -- it touches Rust handlers, routing, templates, and JS.

content.content:
commit be0e44a984989032263c14243323a7dc729fdb2c
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:45:01 2026 -0800

    Add HTMX inline editing for todo titles
    
    Clicking the edit pencil now swaps the todo item with an inline edit
    form via hx-get, without navigating to a separate page. Saving swaps
    back to the updated item view; Cancel restores the original item via
    GET /todos/{id}. Focus management: entering edit mode focuses the
    input, saving/canceling focuses the edit link. No-JS fallback still
    navigates to the full edit page.
    
    New endpoint: GET /todos/{id} returns a single todo item fragment.
    New template: todo_item_edit.html for the inline edit form.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 src/routes/mod.rs             |   3 +-
 src/routes/todos.rs           | 111 +++++++++++++++++---
 src/startup.rs                |   7 +-
 static/js/todo-focus.js       |  57 +++++++---
 templates/todo_item.html      |   1 +
 templates/todo_item_edit.html |  18 ++++
 tests/api/todos.rs            | 238 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 7 files changed, 401 insertions(+), 34 deletions(-)

content.text: Now let me review the tests.

content.content:
commit be0e44a984989032263c14243323a7dc729fdb2c
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:45:01 2026 -0800

    Add HTMX inline editing for todo titles
    
    Clicking the edit pencil now swaps the todo item with an inline edit
    form via hx-get, without navigating to a separate page. Saving swaps
    back to the updated item view; Cancel restores the original item via
    GET /todos/{id}. Focus management: entering edit mode focuses the
    input, saving/canceling focuses the edit link. No-JS fallback still
    navigates to the full edit page.
    
    New endpoint: GET /todos/{id} returns a single todo item fragment.
    New template: todo_item_edit.html for the inline edit form.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/tests/api/todos.rs b/tests/api/todos.rs
index 4eca48a..23454c0 100644
--- a/tests/api/todos.rs
+++ b/tests/api/todos.rs
@@ -1556,3 +1556,241 @@ async fn htmx_post_delete_includes_announce_trigger() {
         "Announcement should mention deletion, got: {hx_trigger}"
     );
 }
+
+// --- HTMX inline editing tests ---
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn htmx_get_edit_returns_inline_edit_form_fragment() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client =
+        register_and_login(&app.address, "htmx_edit@example.com", "securepassword123").await;
+
+    // Add a todo
+    client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .form(&[("title", "Inline edit me")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    // Get the todo ID
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+    let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body);
+
+    // GET edit with HX-Request header should return inline form fragment
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos/{}/edit", &app.address, todo_id))
+        .header("HX-Request", "true")
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get inline edit form");
+
+    assert_eq!(
+        response.status().as_u16(),
+        200,
+        "HTMX edit should return 200"
+    );
+
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+
+    // Should be a fragment, not a full page
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("<!DOCTYPE html>"),
+        "HTMX response should be a fragment, not a full page"
+    );
+
+    // Should contain the inline edit form
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item--editing"),
+        "Should contain the editing state class"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item__edit-input"),
+        "Should contain the edit input"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("Inline edit me"),
+        "Should contain the current title in the input"
+    );
+    assert!(body.contains("Save"), "Should contain a Save button");
+    assert!(body.contains("Cancel"), "Should contain a Cancel button");
+}
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn htmx_post_edit_returns_updated_item_fragment() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client = register_and_login(
+        &app.address,
+        "htmx_edit_save@example.com",
+        "securepassword123",
+    )
+    .await;
+
+    // Add a todo
+    client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .form(&[("title", "Old HTMX title")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    // Get the todo ID
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+    let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body);
+
+    // POST edit with HX-Request header should return updated item fragment
+    let response = client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos/{}/edit", &app.address, todo_id))
+        .header("HX-Request", "true")
+        .form(&[("title", "New HTMX title")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to save inline edit");
+
+    assert_eq!(
+        response.status().as_u16(),
+        200,
+        "HTMX edit save should return 200"
+    );
+
+    // Should have announce trigger
+    let hx_trigger = response
+        .headers()
+        .get("hx-trigger")
+        .expect("HTMX edit response should include HX-Trigger header")
+        .to_str()
+        .unwrap();
+    assert!(
+        hx_trigger.contains("announce"),
+        "Should fire announce event, got: {hx_trigger}"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        hx_trigger.contains("updated"),
+        "Announcement should mention update, got: {hx_trigger}"
+    );
+
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+
+    // Should be a fragment, not a full page
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("<!DOCTYPE html>"),
+        "HTMX response should be a fragment"
+    );
+
+    // Should contain the updated item (not the edit form)
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("New HTMX title"),
+        "Should contain the updated title"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("todo-item--editing"),
+        "Should not be in editing state after save"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item"),
+        "Should contain the todo-item class"
+    );
+}
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn htmx_get_todo_item_returns_item_fragment() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client =
+        register_and_login(&app.address, "htmx_cancel@example.com", "securepassword123").await;
+
+    // Add a todo
+    client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .form(&[("title", "Cancel edit me")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    // Get the todo ID
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+    let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body);
+
+    // GET /todos/{id} should return the item fragment (used by cancel button)
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos/{}", &app.address, todo_id))
+        .header("HX-Request", "true")
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todo item");
+
+    assert_eq!(
+        response.status().as_u16(),
+        200,
+        "GET /todos/{{id}} should return 200"
+    );
+
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+
+    assert!(!body.contains("<!DOCTYPE html>"), "Should be a fragment");
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("Cancel edit me"),
+        "Should contain the todo title"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item"),
+        "Should contain the todo-item element"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("todo-item--editing"),
+        "Should be in view mode, not edit mode"
+    );
+}
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn edit_link_has_htmx_attributes_for_inline_editing() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client = register_and_login(
+        &app.address,
+        "htmx_edit_attrs@example.com",
+        "securepassword123",
+    )
+    .await;
+
+    client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .form(&[("title", "Check attrs")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+
+    // The edit link should have hx-get for inline editing
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("hx-get"),
+        "Edit link should have hx-get attribute for HTMX inline editing"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("hx-target"),
+        "Edit link should have hx-target for swapping the item"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("hx-swap=\"outerHTML\""),
+        "Edit link should swap outerHTML to replace the entire item"
+    );
+}

commit be0e44a984989032263c14243323a7dc729fdb2c
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:45:01 2026 -0800

    Add HTMX inline editing for todo titles
    
    Clicking the edit pencil now swaps the todo item with an inline edit
    form via hx-get, without navigating to a separate page. Saving swaps
    back to the updated item view; Cancel restores the original item via
    GET /todos/{id}. Focus management: entering edit mode focuses the
    input, saving/canceling focuses the edit link. No-JS fallback still
    navigates to the full edit page.
    
    New endpoint: GET /todos/{id} returns a single todo item fragment.
    New template: todo_item_edit.html for the inline edit form.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/tests/api/todos.rs b/tests/api/todos.rs
index 4eca48a..23454c0 100644
--- a/tests/api/todos.rs
+++ b/tests/api/todos.rs
@@ -1556,3 +1556,241 @@ async fn htmx_post_delete_includes_announce_trigger() {
         "Announcement should mention deletion, got: {hx_trigger}"
     );
 }
+
+// --- HTMX inline editing tests ---
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn htmx_get_edit_returns_inline_edit_form_fragment() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client =
+        register_and_login(&app.address, "htmx_edit@example.com", "securepassword123").await;
+
+    // Add a todo
+    client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .form(&[("title", "Inline edit me")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    // Get the todo ID
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+    let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body);
+
+    // GET edit with HX-Request header should return inline form fragment
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos/{}/edit", &app.address, todo_id))
+        .header("HX-Request", "true")
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get inline edit form");
+
+    assert_eq!(
+        response.status().as_u16(),
+        200,
+        "HTMX edit should return 200"
+    );
+
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+
+    // Should be a fragment, not a full page
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("<!DOCTYPE html>"),
+        "HTMX response should be a fragment, not a full page"
+    );
+
+    // Should contain the inline edit form
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item--editing"),
+        "Should contain the editing state class"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item__edit-input"),
+        "Should contain the edit input"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("Inline edit me"),
+        "Should contain the current title in the input"
+    );
+    assert!(body.contains("Save"), "Should contain a Save button");
+    assert!(body.contains("Cancel"), "Should contain a Cancel button");
+}
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn htmx_post_edit_returns_updated_item_fragment() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client = register_and_login(
+        &app.address,
+        "htmx_edit_save@example.com",
+        "securepassword123",
+    )
+    .await;
+
+    // Add a todo
+    client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .form(&[("title", "Old HTMX title")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    // Get the todo ID
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+    let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body);
+
+    // POST edit with HX-Request header should return updated item fragment
+    let response = client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos/{}/edit", &app.address, todo_id))
+        .header("HX-Request", "true")
+        .form(&[("title", "New HTMX title")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to save inline edit");
+
+    assert_eq!(
+        response.status().as_u16(),
+        200,
+        "HTMX edit save should return 200"
+    );
+
+    // Should have announce trigger
+    let hx_trigger = response
+        .headers()
+        .get("hx-trigger")
+        .expect("HTMX edit response should include HX-Trigger header")
+        .to_str()
+        .unwrap();
+    assert!(
+        hx_trigger.contains("announce"),
+        "Should fire announce event, got: {hx_trigger}"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        hx_trigger.contains("updated"),
+        "Announcement should mention update, got: {hx_trigger}"
+    );
+
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+
+    // Should be a fragment, not a full page
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("<!DOCTYPE html>"),
+        "HTMX response should be a fragment"
+    );
+
+    // Should contain the updated item (not the edit form)
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("New HTMX title"),
+        "Should contain the updated title"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("todo-item--editing"),
+        "Should not be in editing state after save"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item"),
+        "Should contain the todo-item class"
+    );
+}
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn htmx_get_todo_item_returns_item_fragment() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client =
+        register_and_login(&app.address, "htmx_cancel@example.com", "securepassword123").await;
+
+    // Add a todo
+    client
+        .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .form(&[("title", "Cancel edit me")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    // Get the todo ID
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+    let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body);
+
+    // GET /todos/{id} should return the item fragment (used by cancel button)
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos/{}", &app.address, todo_id))
+        .header("HX-Request", "true")
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todo item");
+
+    assert_eq!(
+        response.status().as_u16(),
+        200,
+        "GET /todos/{{id}} should return 200"
+    );
+
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+
+    assert!(!body.contains("<!DOCTYPE html>"), "Should be a fragment");
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("Cancel edit me"),
+        "Should contain the todo title"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("todo-item"),
+        "Should contain the todo-item element"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        !body.contains("todo-item--editing"),
+        "Should be in view mode, not edit mode"
+    );
+}
+
+#[tokio::test]
+async fn edit_link_has_htmx_attributes_for_inline_editing() {
+    let app = spawn_app().await;
+    let client = register_and_login(
+        &app.address,
+        "htmx_edit_attrs@example.com",
+        "securepassword123",
+    )
+    .await;
+
+    client

+        .form(&[("title", "Check attrs")])
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to add todo");
+
+    let response = client
+        .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address))
+        .send()
+        .await
+        .expect("Failed to get todos page");
+    let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
+
+    // The edit link should have hx-get for inline editing

+        body.contains("hx-get"),
+        "Edit link should have hx-get attribute for HTMX inline editing"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("hx-target"),
+        "Edit link should have hx-target for swapping the item"
+    );
+    assert!(
+        body.contains("hx-swap=\"outerHTML\""),
+        "Edit link should swap outerHTML to replace the entire item"
+    );
+}

content.text: Good, the helper exists. Let me now also verify the full current state of the routes/todos.rs to ensure no issues with the new `get_todo_item` handler.

I have a thorough picture now. Let me also quickly check something -- the `post_edit_todo` HTMX path does an extra DB read after a successful update to return the item fragment. Let me verify this pattern.

input.content:
**Review of be0e44a -- HTMX inline editing for todo titles**

**I consent.**

This is the largest commit in this batch (7 files, 401 lines added). Detailed review by area:

---

**Rust changes (routes/todos.rs, startup.rs, routes/mod.rs)**

1. **New `get_todo_item` handler** (lines 355-384): Clean, follows the established pattern. Auth check, ownership guard, 403/404 for edge cases. Returns `TodoItemTemplate` fragment. Used by the Cancel button's `hx-get="/todos/{id}"`. This is a proper REST-style resource endpoint -- `GET /todos/{id}` returns a single item. Good API design.

2. **`get_edit_todo` now returns different templates for HTMX vs non-HTMX**: HTMX gets `TodoItemEditTemplate` (inline fragment), non-HTMX gets `EditTodoTemplate` (full page). Same handler, same URL -- follows our convention of one handler checking the `HX-Request` header. Correct.

3. **`post_edit_todo` HTMX path does an extra DB read** (lines 295-300): After `update_todo_title` succeeds, it re-fetches the item to build the `TodoItemTemplate` response. This is necessary because `update_todo_title` returns `Ok(())`, not the updated item. The extra query is cheap (primary key lookup) and the alternative would be changing the service signature, which would be a larger refactor. Pragmatic choice. The `None` fallback at line 309 handles the unlikely race condition where the item is deleted between update and fetch by redirecting.

4. **HTMX validation error path**: Returns `TodoItemEditTemplate` (the inline form) with 422, keeping the user in edit mode. Non-HTMX still returns the full `EditTodoTemplate`. However, I note that the **HTMX error path does not pass the error message** to `TodoItemEditTemplate` -- the template struct only has `todo_id` and `current_title`, no `error_message` field. This means if a user submits a title that is too long via HTMX, they get the form back but with no visible error message. The `user_facing` variable is computed but never used in the HTMX branch. This is a **minor gap** -- but since the `<input>` has `maxlength="300"` which prevents client-side overflows, and the only other validation error is empty title which has `required` on the input, this is unlikely to be hit in practice. I will not block on this but flag it for a follow-up.

5. **Route registration** (startup.rs line 66): `.route("/todos/{id}", axum::routing::get(get_todo_item))` -- correctly placed before the more specific `/todos/{id}/toggle`, `/todos/{id}/delete`, `/todos/{id}/edit` routes. Axum's router matches by specificity, so this won't shadow the sub-routes.

---

**Template changes**

6. **`todo_item_edit.html`**: Clean inline edit form. The `<li>` preserves the same `id="todo-{{ todo_id }}"` as the view mode, which is critical for `hx-target` and `hx-swap="outerHTML"` to work correctly. Has `autofocus` on the input. `method="post" action="/todos/{id}/edit"` provides the no-JS fallback. `hx-post` on the form and `hx-get` on Cancel -- both target the same `#todo-{id}` with `outerHTML`. Correct.

7. **`todo_item.html`**: Added `hx-get="/todos/{id}/edit" hx-target="#todo-{id}" hx-swap="outerHTML"` to the edit link. The `href` remains for no-JS fallback. HTMX intercepts the click. Locality of behavior -- the element is self-describing.

---

**JavaScript changes (todo-focus.js)**

8. **Refactored `pendingFocusTarget` to use typed objects**: `{ type: "element", el: ... }` vs `{ type: "selector", selector: ... }`. The selector approach is needed because the inline edit form replaces the DOM element, so a direct element reference would become stale. Using a selector that queries the DOM after settle is the correct pattern.

9. **Edit mode detection**: Checks `responseText.indexOf("todo-item--editing")` to detect entering edit mode, and `responseText.indexOf("todo-item__edit")` without `todo-item--editing` for exiting. This is content-sniffing, which is fragile in principle, but pragmatic here since we control both the response and the detection. The class names are stable conventions.

10. **Focus targets are correct**: Entering edit mode focuses `.todo-item__edit-input` (the text input). Exiting edit mode (save or cancel) focuses `.todo-item__edit` (the edit pencil link). This follows WCAG -- focus goes to the logical place after a DOM change.

---

**Tests (4 new, 65 total)**

11. All four new tests are well-structured:
   - `htmx_get_edit_returns_inline_edit_form_fragment`: Verifies HTMX GET returns fragment (no DOCTYPE), contains editing class, input, title, Save, Cancel.
   - `htmx_post_edit_returns_updated_item_fragment`: Verifies save returns item fragment with updated title, HX-Trigger announce header, not in editing state.
   - `htmx_get_todo_item_returns_item_fragment`: Verifies the new `GET /todos/{id}` endpoint returns item fragment (used by Cancel).
   - `edit_link_has_htmx_attributes_for_inline_editing`: Verifies the edit link in the todos page has `hx-get`, `hx-target`, `hx-swap="outerHTML"`.

All tests follow our naming convention (behavior specifications) and use the established `spawn_app` + `register_and_login` pattern.

---

**Minor observation (non-blocking)**:
- The HTMX validation error path in `post_edit_todo` computes `user_facing` but does not include it in the `TodoItemEditTemplate`. The inline form will re-render without an error message. This is mitigated by `maxlength` and `required` attributes on the input, but could be addressed in a follow-up if we want server-side validation feedback in the inline form.

No blocking concerns. I consent to this change.
input.summary: Consent on be0e44a - inline editing reviewed thoroughly

---

9. **Edit mode detection**: Checks `responseText.indexOf("todo-item--editing")` to detect entering edit mode, and `responseText.indexOf("todo-item__edit")` without `todo-item--editing` for exiting. **Tests (4 new, 65 total)**

11. All four new tests are well-structured:
   - `htmx_get_edit_returns_inline_edit_form_fragment`: Verifies HTMX GET returns fragment (no DOCTYPE), contains editing class, input, title, Save, Cancel.
   - `htmx_post_edit_returns_updated_item_fragment`: Verifies save returns item fragment with updated title, HX-Trigger announce header, not in editing state.
   - `htmx_get_todo_item_returns_item_fragment`: Verifies the new `GET /todos/{id}` endpoint returns item fragment (used by Cancel).
   - `edit_link_has_htmx_attributes_for_inline_editing`: Verifies the edit link in the todos page has `hx-get`, `hx-target`, `hx-swap="outerHTML"`.

All tests follow our naming convention (behavior specifications) and use the established `spawn_app` + `register_and_login` pattern.

- The HTMX validation error path in `post_edit_todo` computes `user_facing` but does not include it in the `TodoItemEditTemplate`. The inline form will re-render without an error message.   }
}

   - `htmx_get_edit_returns_inline_edit_form_fragment`: Verifies HTMX GET returns fragment (no DOCTYPE), contains editing class, input, title, Save, Cancel.
   - `htmx_post_edit_returns_updated_item_fragment`: Verifies save returns item fragment with updated title, HX-Trigger announce header, not in editing state.
   - `htmx_get_todo_item_returns_item_fragment`: Verifies the new `GET /todos/{id}` endpoint returns item fragment (used by Cancel).
   - `edit_link_has_htmx_attributes_for_inline_editing`: Verifies the edit link in the todos page has `hx-get`, `hx-target`, `hx-swap="outerHTML"`.

All tests follow our naming convention (behavior specifications) and use the established `spawn_app` + `register_and_login` pattern.

---

**Minor observation (non-blocking)**:
- The HTMX validation error path in `post_edit_todo` computes `user_facing` but does not include it in the `TodoItemEditTemplate`. The inline form will re-render without an error message. This is mitigated by `maxlength` and `required` attributes on the input, but could be addressed in a follow-up if we want server-side validation feedback in the inline form.

No blocking concerns. I consent to this change.

content.text:
Consent sent for Task #5 (be0e44a). This is the final task in Carson's driving phase. 

Summary of my review: The implementation is solid across all layers -- proper HTMX fragment/full-page branching in Rust handlers, new `GET /todos/{id}` resource endpoint, inline edit template with correct DOM ID preservation, and focus management via selector-based targeting. One non-blocking observation: the HTMX validation error path does not surface the error message in the inline form template, though client-side `maxlength` and `required` mitigate this in practice. Standing by for consensus count.
